description: Conflicts between the designer-authored token system and production-extracted values. Each ticket is an actionable gap for engineering.

tickets.title: Status color: Success
tickets.action: Engineering to update success color to #00B67A across all surfaces.

tickets.title: Status color: Error
tickets.action: Engineering to update error color to #FF5C5C across all surfaces.

tickets.title: Status color: Info
tickets.action: Engineering to update info color to #33CCFF. Note: #0d47a1 flagged previously as Material Blue 900 library leak — this confirms it should be replaced.

tickets.title: Status color: Warning
tickets.action: Engineering to add #FF7C35 as warning color. No close match exists in production.

tickets.title: Brand primary color — FIVE-WAY conflict
tickets.action: Frozen at #4e3bc2 as working canonical pending brand team formal override. Migration work proceeds. Brand team must confirm or override. Codemod must touch ~138 files.
tickets.notes: FIVE-WAY conflict confirmed by deep extraction (2026-04-28). Designer: #722ED1 (1 use). Figma canonical: #4e3bc2 (218 uses / 112 files). In-progress: #6651e4 (15 uses). Typo: #4d3bc2 (24 uses). 5th variant: #4a3fb4 (11 uses). Brand team confirmation required before ANY codemod runs.

tickets.title: Icon system not implemented
tickets.designerValue: 1,208 SVG icons across 22 categories (line + fill variants)
tickets.action: Engineering to replace image-based icons with SVG icon system. Migrate to lucide-react or custom SVG sprite using designer's icon library as source. Multi-sprint engineering effort.

tickets.title: Typography scale gap
tickets.action: Resolved — added font/heading/sm (24px), font/heading/md (32px), font/heading/lg (40px), font/heading/xl (56px) to canonical via manual-canonicals.json. Engineering: ensure Nunito loads correctly at these sizes.

tickets.title: Brand primary typo — #4d3bc2 shipping in 46 files
tickets.action: Engineering to find-replace #4d3bc2 with #4e3bc2 across student dashboard repo. No design decision needed — this is a typo, not an intentional variant. 46 files, ~80 occurrences.
tickets.notes: Invisible to the human eye (ΔE 0.5) but wrong in every computed style. Will cause confusion once CSS variables are introduced. Fix before codemod runs. Source-code evidence in docs/repo-analysis-student-dashboard.md.

tickets.title: Progress success color not in ledger
tickets.action: Engineering to update progress completed fill to #00B67A (color/success/500). Also used as $success-green and $completed-state in dashboard _variables.scss.
tickets.notes: DC-001 addresses the global success color. This is the component-specific instance on ProgressBar. Both should resolve together when DC-001 ships.

tickets.title: Paused state color not in ledger
tickets.action: PENDING DESIGN CONFIRMATION — token added as provisional, unblocks engineering. Design team to confirm color/info/400 (#3b9af5) or override.
tickets.notes: Provisionally added as color/info/400 in manual-canonicals.json at confidence 0.6 (candidate tier). Replaces the auto-extracted #15e8ff that previously occupied that slot. Now visible at /foundations/color/ in the Info group. Engineering can codemod the ProgressBar paused variant against this token; rename happens automatically if design overrides.

tickets.title: Danger/error color mismatch — Button
tickets.action: Engineering to update danger button bg to #FF5C5C across all Button danger variants.
tickets.notes: Found in button.module.scss (.danger variant border + text). Soft coral (#ff8480) ships instead of the design system error red (#FF5C5C). Surfaced during Tier 2 component build 2026-04-28.

tickets.title: Info/teal color mismatch — Button (HIGH)
tickets.action: Engineering to update info button to #33CCFF. Current production value (#60bfbd) is teal, not cyan — a significant perceptual difference.
tickets.notes: Found in button.module.scss (.info variant border + text). HIGH severity mismatch — different hue family entirely (cyan vs teal-green), not just a shade difference. Surfaced during Tier 2 component build 2026-04-28.

tickets.title: Dominant dark-gray #384655 has no token
tickets.action: Add color/neutral/850 = #384655 to ledger and library. Engineering to replace 70 hardcoded instances across 27 files.
tickets.notes: Biggest unmapped color by usage. Lives between neutral/700 (#2D3D4D) and neutral/600 (#3D4D5D). Used as dark text, icon color, border across many components. Token added 2026-04-28.

tickets.title: Title near-black #222a33 has no token
tickets.action: Add color/neutral/950 = #222a33. Engineering to replace 45 instances across 22 files.
tickets.notes: Used for H3 title text across the dashboard. Darker than neutral/900 (#0D1D2D). Verify if this should map to neutral/900 (ΔE ~8) or get its own step. Token added 2026-04-28.

tickets.title: Light purple #8a78f9 not in canonical ledger
tickets.action: Add as color/primary/250 or map to primary/300. Currently exists as $text-light-purple in _variables.scss but not in the DS ledger.
tickets.notes: 33 uses across 17 files. Used for secondary brand text, active pill labels, subtle brand accents. Token added 2026-04-28 as color/primary/250.

tickets.title: App background lavender tint #f5f4fa not in ledger
tickets.action: Add as color/surface/app-bg. This is the main DashboardLayout background — it has a subtle lavender tint that neutral/50 (#F9FAFB) does not capture.
tickets.notes: 20 uses across 13 files. $app-background in _variables.scss. The tint comes from the purple brand color bleeding into the background — intentional or drift? Token added 2026-04-28 with semantic alias surface/bg/app.

tickets.title: Two unmapped yellows — neither matches secondary/500
tickets.action: Resolve: do these map to secondary/300 (#FFE266) and secondary/400 (#FFD833) respectively, or are they independent values? Engineering audit needed.
tickets.notes: #ffc200 (16 uses) and #ffd02b (12 uses) appear in reward UI, badges, and highlight contexts. Both are close to existing secondary scale steps but not exact. Tokens added 2026-04-28 as color/secondary/350 (#ffc200) and color/secondary/250 (#ffd02b).

tickets.title: Fifth brand purple variant #4a3fb4 discovered
tickets.action: DC-005 resolution will determine if this retires to the canonical. Until then: add as color/primary/450 with conflict note.
tickets.notes: 11 uses across 5 files. DC-005 is now a FIVE-way conflict: #722ED1 / #4e3bc2 / #6651e4 / #4d3bc2 / #4a3fb4. Token added 2026-04-28 as color/primary/450 with DC-018 description in Figma.

tickets.title: Utility grays missing from token system
tickets.action: Add color/neutral/450 ($text-disabled), color/neutral/650 ($notstarted-state-dark), color/neutral/750 ($text-gray) to ledger and library.
tickets.notes: These exist as SCSS variables in _variables.scss but were never added to the canonical ledger. Combined 47 uses across 37 files. Tokens added 2026-04-28.

tickets.title: Bootstrap blue leak in TextTruncate
tickets.action: Engineering to remove #007bff from TextTruncate. Replace with var(--text-info) or var(--text-brand), or remove entirely.
tickets.notes: Bootstrap default blue found in newDashboard atom TextTruncate. Not a BrightChamps brand color. Classic library leak. Atom also uses 5 other Bootstrap blues (#0056b3, #3182ce, #63b3ed, #6c757d, #a0aec0) — all need removing.

tickets.title: newDashboard ProgressBar uses traffic-light colors
tickets.action: Align newDashboard ProgressBar to use color/success/500, color/warning/500, color/error/500. Currently uses three non-canonical traffic-light values.
tickets.notes: Two parallel ProgressBar implementations confirmed. Legacy (src/components/ProgressBar) uses brand-purple. newDashboard atom uses traffic-light palette. Decision needed: which is canonical for which context?

tickets.title: Material Design purples in newDashboard molecules
tickets.action: Audit all Material purple variants in newDashboard components. Replace with semantic/color brand tokens.
tickets.notes: Surfaced during newDashboard molecule audit. Material Design purples (#673AB7 = Material Deep Purple 500, #7C4DFF = Material Deep Purple A200) leaking through molecules. Found in InstallmentCard, UpgradeCardFeed, MakeupStatusModal, ChatBot.

tickets.title: 7+ red variants — no canonical error red
tickets.action: Consolidate all red variants to color/error/500 (#FF5C5C). 7 distinct reds across the codebase means every team member chose their own. Run targeted codemod per file group after DC-005 resolves.
tickets.notes: Most impactful color proliferation issue after the brand purple conflict. Every error/danger state uses a different red. DC-002 covers global error color; this ticket is the full sprawl.

tickets.title: 5+ green variants — success color fragmented
tickets.action: Consolidate all green variants to color/success/500. #43a047 is a confirmed Material Design green leak. #238b2e appears in LessonList (DC-014).
tickets.notes: 5 distinct greens across the codebase. #43a047 (Material Design Green 600) is a library leak. DC-001 and DC-009 address specific instances — this ticket covers the full sprawl.

tickets.title: 5+ blue variants including Bootstrap leak
tickets.action: Consolidate info blues to color/info/500 (#33CCFF). Remove Bootstrap (#007bff) and Material (#0d47a1) leaks. Map remaining variants to nearest semantic.
tickets.notes: Blues are the most fragmented color family. DC-003 covers #0d47a1 (Material Blue 900). DC-012 covers #60bfbd. DC-020 covers #007bff. DC-030 covers parent-hub electric blues. This ticket is the umbrella consolidation.

tickets.title: 5+ yellow variants — secondary color fragmented
tickets.action: Consolidate yellows to color/secondary scale. Map each variant to nearest secondary/* step. Most should map to secondary/400 (#FFD833) or secondary/500 (#FFCE00).
tickets.notes: DC-017 covers the two most-used rogue yellows. This ticket covers the full 5+ variant sprawl — including warm yellows in Calendar (#fff5dc, #ffe895).

tickets.title: 7+ orange/brown variants — warning color fragmented
tickets.action: Consolidate oranges to color/warning/500 (#FF7C35). #d0a044 is a gold/orange-border value — may be accent. #f9cf53 is communication course color — keep separate.
tickets.notes: Orange is the second most fragmented warm color. Skipped-state, warning-state, and accent all use different oranges.

tickets.title: TruncateText and TextTruncate — duplicate atoms
tickets.productionValue: two separate components doing the same thing
tickets.action: Engineering to deprecate one. Recommend keeping TruncateText (cleaner name, no Bootstrap leak) and migrating all TextTruncate call sites.
tickets.notes: Both exist in newDashboard/components/atoms. TextTruncate has Bootstrap leak (#007bff). TruncateText appears cleaner. Need usage count per file.

tickets.title: Tailwind gray classes in parent-hub
tickets.productionValue: Tailwind utility classes mixing with SCSS modules
tickets.action: Audit parent-hub SCSS for Tailwind class mixing. Parent-hub appears to use both Tailwind (#111827, #374151, #6b7280 = gray-900/700/500) and SCSS modules simultaneously — inconsistent with rest of codebase.
tickets.notes: Surfaced during parent-hub inventory. 53 SCSS modules + Tailwind-style hex values creates two styling systems in one surface.

tickets.title: Electric blues in parent-hub (#1d3bff, #3273dc family)
tickets.action: Parent-hub uses a family of deep electric blues not found in the student dashboard or the token system. Determine if parent-hub has a different brand palette or if these are leaks.
tickets.notes: Parent-hub (99 unique colors, zero in library) may have been designed against a different token system or no token system at all.

tickets.title: Near-black sprawl — 5+ values for text/heading
tickets.action: Consolidate near-blacks to color/neutral/900 or color/neutral/950. #1b0742 is a purple-tinted near-black that may be intentional (dark brand moment) — verify.
tickets.notes: 5 near-black values across the codebase. DC-014 covers #222a33 (45 uses). This ticket covers the full sprawl of dark values, plus 10+ near-blacks (#181818, #191919, #1a1a1a, etc.) found in parent-hub.
